mapping.a_element: symplectic cohomology as an algebraic invariant attached to a symplectic/contact manifold
mapping.b_element: the scaled ordered K_0-group as an algebraic invariant attached to the tiling's C*-algebra

brief_justification: The only overlap is the generic pattern of assigning an algebraic invariant to a geometric object; the actual machinery differs entirely. Paper A uses Floer-theoretic symplectic cohomology (Hamiltonian orbits, holomorphic curves) for classification in symplectic/contact topology, whereas Paper B uses operator-algebraic noncommutative geometry (a groupoid C*-algebra of a tiling and its K_0-group) to label spectral gaps of Schroedinger operators via the integrated density of states — there is no role-for-role correspondence between Floer theory and operator K-theory.
